title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan targets the top-level validation of CCF (Central Control Fabric) power management (PM) flows within the SOC. It covers coordinated power state transitions, resource arbitration, stress detection and response, license handshake and arbitration, policy-driven flow control, and system-level observability and debug. CCF integrates with PMA, Punit, and core modules to orchestrate power state changes (C-states, frequency/voltage scaling), manage exclusive access to shared resources (MLC flush, IOSF channels), and enforce system policies. Key interactions include cross-module synchronization, telemetry-driven stress handling, license negotiation, and debug visibility, ensuring robust and safe PM flow operation under normal and stress conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: This test plan encompasses validation of: (1) coordinated state transitions and resource arbitration across cores/modules, including exclusive access scenarios; (2) telemetry and AI-based stress detection and response mechanisms for electrical, thermal, and power virus events; (3) license handshake and arbitration flows between PMA, Punit, and cores; (4) policy-driven flow control and ownership prioritization; (5) system-level observability and debug mechanisms for PM flows, stress events, and arbitration outcomes. The plan addresses normal operation, stress scenarios, contention, race conditions, and system recovery. It does not cover lower-level signal integrity or individual IP-specific PM flows outside CCF orchestration.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: All features require access to SOC-level debug and telemetry infrastructure, programmable stress injection capabilities, and synchronized PMA/Punit/core interfaces. Test environment must support controlled stress event generation, license arbitration triggers, and policy configuration. Observability tools must be available for real-time status monitoring and debug.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Potential security implications include unauthorized PM flow initiation, license spoofing, and denial-of-service via resource contention. Mitigations involve policy gating, license arbitration, and observability mechanisms to detect and prevent malicious or unintended transitions. Validation will ensure that security/performance policies are enforced and that flow ownership is correctly prioritized under stress and error conditions.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of simultaneous stress events, false positives in stress detection, and loss of debug visibility under high contention. Contingencies involve fallback flows, enhanced observability, and additional stress scenario coverage to ensure robustness and survivability.

title: CCF Power Management Flows: Top-Level Validation
subsection content: Prerequisites include validated PMA/Punit/core interfaces, functional telemetry and debug infrastructure, and programmable policy and license configuration. Stress injection tools and observability mechanisms must be operational prior to test execution.

title: Stress Detection and Response Mechanisms
subsection content: Validate telemetry and AI-based detection of electrical, thermal, and power virus stress events. Trigger stress scenarios (simultaneous transitions, abnormal sensor readings) and verify that CCF/PMA responds with appropriate prevention actions (throttling, staggered transitions, aborts), including fallback and survivability flows. Observe system recovery and learning from false positives.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: Validate PMA thermal event handling, sticky LOG bit behavior, and throttling response to simulated thermal excursions.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: Simulate thermal events by toggling THERM_STATUS_UPDATE bits, observe per-core THERM_STATUS LOG bits, and verify PMA throttling actions.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: Randomly toggle THERM_STATUS_UPDATE bits, including simultaneous assertion of multiple bits, to cover complex stress scenarios.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: PMA telemetry enabled, THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register access, CLPMA_CR_THROTTLE_CONFIG and PMA_CR_FORCE_THROTTLE_CONTROL for throttling, per-core THERM_STATUS register observability.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: Sample all thermal event bits, verify sticky LOG behavior, and check recovery after event clearance.

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation

title: Thermal Stress Detection and Throttling Response Validation
subsection content: Observability of LOG bits, ability to clear LOG bits via Ucode, and debug registers for event tracking.

title: Simulated Thermal Event Trigger and LOG Bit Validation
subsection content: Configure the system with all cores active and PMA telemetry enabled. Write to THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register at address 0xA204 to simulate thermal events by toggling bits such as THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS, PROCHOT_STATUS, OUT_OF_SPEC_STATUS, THRESHOLD1_STATUS, THRESHOLD2_STATUS, POWER_LIMITATION_STATUS, CURRENT_LIMIT_STATUS, and CROSS_DOMAIN_LIMIT_STATUS. Observe per-core THERM_STATUS registers for correct sticky LOG bit behavior. Ensure that LOG bits are set on event transitions and only cleared by Ucode writes.

title: Simulated Thermal Event Trigger and LOG Bit Validation
subsection content: 1. Enable PMA telemetry and ensure all cores are active. 2. Write to THERM_STATUS_UPDATE register to assert each thermal event bit individually and in combinations. 3. Sample per-core THERM_STATUS registers after each event assertion. 4. Clear events by writing '0' to the corresponding bits and observe LOG bit clearing via Ucode. 5. Repeat for all event bits and combinations.

title: Simulated Thermal Event Trigger and LOG Bit Validation
subsection content: Pass if LOG bits are set on event transitions and cleared only by Ucode writes. Fail if LOG bits do not behave as specified or if events are not properly reflected in THERM_STATUS registers. HW errors include incorrect LOG bit behavior or register update failures.

title: Simulated Thermal Event Trigger and LOG Bit Validation
subsection content: Use PMA telemetry logs, per-core THERM_STATUS register dumps, and Visa/PMON to confirm correct LOG bit behavior.

title: Simulated Thermal Event Trigger and LOG Bit Validation
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. PVT checkers for thermal event handling and LOG bit stickiness.

subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS latest version, PMON for register monitoring, PythonSV for scripting register writes and reads, Supercollider for stress patterns.

subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with PMA telemetry enabled, OS kernel with register access utilities, drivers for PMA and thermal monitoring.

Monitor PMA triggers for throttling actions such as clock modulation and frequency reduction via CLPMA_CR_THROTTLE_CONFIG and PMA_CR_FORCE_THROTTLE_CONTROL. Verify system recovery when events are cleared.

title: Throttling Response to Complex Thermal Excursions
subsection content: Pass if throttling is engaged for each asserted event and system recovers when events are cleared. Fail if throttling does not occur or system fails to recover. HW errors include missed throttling triggers or recovery failures.

title: System Stability Verification Under Randomized Stress Scenarios
subsection content: Randomize number of participating cores and timing of stress event transitions to cover diverse didt scenarios. Monitor system stability and verify that no simultaneous high-stress transitions occur across modules.

title: System Stability Verification Under Randomized Stress Scenarios
subsection content: 1. Enable PMA and telemetry on all cores/modules. 2. Randomize number and timing of stress event transitions. 3. Monitor system stability and transition ordering. 4. Verify no simultaneous high-stress transitions across modules. 5. Repeat for different randomization scenarios.

title: System Stability Verification Under Randomized Stress Scenarios
subsection content: Pass if system remains stable and transitions are staggered. Fail if instability or simultaneous transitions occur. HW errors include instability or missed staggering.
